content: The relative performance gap between intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM), random network distillation (RND), and standalone curious agents can be explained by whether they integrate 2024–2025 SOTA structured episodic memory for active learning: curious agents that explicitly tie curiosity-driven exploration to SOTA gradient-based associative memory outperform both ICM and RND, because they retain and reuse rare surprising states instead of discarding prediction errors after updating reward weights. This means the long-debated performance difference between popular curiosity-driven RL methods is not an inherent flaw in the intrinsic reward design itself, but a function of missing active memory scaffolding.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
